title: dipterocarpaceae
intro: The dipterocarpaceae is a family of flowering plants, specifically a rank in the taxonomic classification of plants that includes approximately 500 species. This family is commonly known as the "drought-tolerant" or "tropical hardwood" family due to the unique characteristics of its members, such as the ability to survive in poor soil conditions. The dipterocarpaceae family is found in tropical regions of Asia and Australia, with many species being highly valued for their timber and medicinal properties. Members of this family, such as the dipterocarp trees, are known for their distinctive broad leaves and ability to thrive in challenging environments.
